action.reasoning: Observation: The current best test balanced_accuracy is 0.8539 from iteration 16 (ensemble_fine.pt). Training new models (ResNet50, Focal Loss ConvNeXt) has failed to improve the ensemble. Analysis: The best models suffer from classifier weight bias typical of long-tailed natural-sampling training. Tau-normalization (dividing each class logit by ||w_c||^tau) is a zero-cost post-hoc method from the decoupling literature that directly corrects classifier weight magnitudes, often improving balanced accuracy significantly. I will apply tau-normalization to the two existing ConvNeXt checkpoints (best_iter9.pt and convnext_s2.pt), grid-search tau_norm and logit-adjustment temperature on the validation set, then re-run the TTA-aware ensemble search on the normalized models. This requires no training and could push the score above 0.8539. Decision: Write tune_tau_norm.py to bake tau-normalization into checkpoint state dicts, then chain tuning for both models, ensemble grid search, smoke test, and eval submission.
action.command: python tune_tau_norm.py --ckpt checkpoints/best_iter9.pt --output checkpoints/best_iter9_tn.pt && python tune_tau_norm.py --ckpt checkpoints/convnext_s2.pt --output checkpoints/convnext_s2_tn.pt && python tune_ensemble_fine.py --ckpt1 checkpoints/best_iter9_tn.pt --ckpt2 checkpoints/convnext_s2_tn.pt --output checkpoints/ensemble_tn.pt && mkdir -p fake_test && cp /data/val.pt fake_test/test.pt && python predict.py --data_path fake_test --checkpoint checkpoints/ensemble_tn.pt --output fake_out.json
